title: FO76 Animation Archive Deep Dive
content:
# FO76 Animation Archive Deep Dive

**Source:** `SeventySix - Animations.ba2` (369MB)
**Total Files:** 31,432 (21,913 .hkx animation files + 9,519 .txt animation data files)
**Archive Type:** GNRL v1

---

## 1. High-Level Breakdown

### File Counts by Actor Type (Top 30)

| Actor | Files | Notes |
|-------|-------|-------|
| character (player/human NPCs) | 8,895 | 3,087 first-person + 6,092 third-person |
| powerarmor | 2,471 | Full separate skeleton with all weapon/emote mirrors |
| supermutant | 735 | Includes cover system animations |
| scorched | 725 | Cover system + weapon overrides |
| createabot (custom robots) | 613 | Assaultron, Protectron, Sentrybot |
| dlc03 (Far Harbor) | 570 | Angler, Fog Crawler, Gulper, Hermit, Rad Rabbit/Chicken |
| dogmeat | 429 | Full companion AI with swimming |
| dlc04 (Nuka-World) | 399 | Cave Cricket, Swarm, Animatronic, Bloodworm |
| dlc01 (Automatron) | 354 | Robot creation expansion |
| moleminer | 298 | Cover system animations present |
| feralghoul | 167 | Multiple ambush types |
| mirelurkking | 129 | Swimming animations |
| deathclaw | 115 | Includes car-flipping animation |
| radstag | 114 | |
| mirelurkqueen | 108 | |
| wendigo | 105 | |
| sheepsquatch | 104 | Quill attacks, back kicks, lunges |
| mirelurk | 104 | |
| mothman | 103 | Teleport, curse, blessing, stalking |
| yaoguai | 101 | Kill-move paired animations |
| robot (generic) | 97 | |
| radscorpion | 92 | |
| molerat | 92 | |
| mirelurkhunter | 91 | |
| snallygaster | 85 | |
| _testcharacter | 81 | **Dev test rig** |
| honeybeast | 81 | |
| alien | 81 | Full combat set |
| megasloth | 79 | |
| mosquito (bloodbug) | 78 | Paired human siphon kill-moves |

### Unique/Rare Actors

| Actor | Files | Significance |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| stormboss | 56 | **Tempest boss** - coil gun + lightning attacks |
| wendigocolossus | 51 | Earle Williams boss - fear/summon head attacks |
| trogg | 52 | The Pitt creatures |
| radbeaver | 32 | Newer critter addition |
| frog | 34 | Critter with 4 idle variants |
| fox | 34 | West Virginia wildlife critter |
| owl | 25 | Combat-capable (dodges, evades, attacks) |
| ultraciteabomination | 23 | Fissure emerge/retreat animations |
| radpheasant | 17 | |
| firefly | 16 | |
| vertibot | 14 | Cargo claw release animation |
| libertyprime | 10 | Full behavior set - gantry exit, agitator insert |
| radhog | 7 | **Skeleton + behaviors only, NO animations** |
| wanamingo | 2 | **Skeleton only** - classic Fallout 2 creature |
| zetaninvader | 2 | Spawn-in + alien rifle - minimal |
| vulture | 1 | Skeleton only |
| cat_pet | 1 | Skeleton only - planned pet system? |
| bipedrobot | 1 | Skeleton only |

---

## 2. Cover System Animations (The Big Find)

**Total cover animations: 284 files across 5 actor types.**

This is a COMPLETE cover-based shooter system that was built and then cut. The animations prove it was fully functional, not just prototyped.

### Cover Actors

| Actor | Cover Anims | Notes |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| character (player) | 177 | Standing, kneeling, left/right sides, weapon-specific |
| scorched | 40 | Full NPC cover AI |
| supermutant | 40 | Hunting rifle + machine gun cover |
| moleminer | 19 | Gripassault cover set |
| powerarmor | 8 | Covert sidearm only |

### Cover Animation Types Present

**Standing Cover (Right Side):**
- `riflecoverstandingrightentertrans` - enter cover from right
- `riflecoverstandingrightexittrans` - leave cover from right
- `riflecoverstandingidle` - idle behind cover
- `riflecoverstandingidleshuffleforward` - shuffle along cover
- `riflecoverstandingidlerighttolefttrans` - switch sides
- `riflecoverstandingrightfiresingleblind` - blind fire single shot
- `riflecoverstandingrightfireautoblind` - blind fire automatic
- `riflecoverstandingrightfireautoblindtrans` / `transrev` - transition in/out of blind fire

**Sighted (Peeking):**
- `riflecoverstandingidlesightedtrans` - transition to aimed
- `riflecoverstandingsightedfiresingle` - aimed single shot from cover
- `riflecoverstandingsightedfireauto` - aimed auto fire from cover
- `riflecoverstandingsightedgrenadethrow` - throw grenade from cover
- `riflecoverstandingsightedgrenadethrowleft` - left-hand grenade
- `riflecoverstandingidlesightedshuffleforward` - aimed shuffling

**Kneeling Cover:**
- `rifleidlereadycoverrightkneel` - kneel behind cover
- `rifleidlereadycoverrightkneelshuffleforward` - shuffle while kneeling
- `riflereloadcoverrightkneel` - reload while kneeling
- `riflestandingcovertocrouchcover` / `riflecrouchcovertostandingcover` - stand/crouch transitions

**Movement While in Cover:**
- `walkforwardcoverright` - walk forward in cover
- `walkbackcoverright` - walk backward in cover
- `walkrightcoverright` - walk right in cover
- `riflecrouchwalkforwardcoverright` - crouch walk forward
- `riflecrouchwalkbackcoverright` - crouch walk back
- `riflecrouchwalkrightcoverright` - crouch walk right
- `sneakwpnentercover` - enter cover from sneak

**Cover Entry:**
- `sneakwpnentercover` - sneak into cover
- `rifleidlereadycovermirrortrans` - mirror/switch sides

**Weapon-Specific Cover:**
- Pistol: Full cover set (23 anims)
- Grip Rifle Straight: Full cover set (27 anims)
- Hunting Rifle: Cover reload variants
- Laser Musket: Cover reload variants
- Laser Rifle (straight + rapid): Cover reload variants
- Pipe Rifle Pistol: Cover reload variants
- Double Barrel Shotgun: Cover reload variants

**Covert Sidearm (separate weapon class):**
- Full 1st-person weapon set (41 anims) - jiggle, fire, sight, reload, melee, sprint
- 3rd-person assembly/fire/reload
- Power armor covert sidearm variants
- This is a dedicated stealth pistol weapon class

### Assessment

The cover system was **production-ready**. Evidence:
1. Player animations in both 1st and 3rd person
2. Three enemy types had full cover AI (scorched, super mutant, mole miner)
3. Multiple weapon types supported
4. Standing AND kneeling variants
5. Blind fire AND aimed fire from cover
6. Grenade throwing from cover
7. Movement along cover walls
8. Transition animations between all states
9. Cover reload animations per weapon type

This was likely cut because it fundamentally changed FO76's combat pacing and would have made PvP dramatically different. May have been part of Nuclear Winter's original design.

---

## 3. Mothman Animation Set (Cryptid Analysis)

**103 animations total** - one of the most complex creature rigs in the game.

### Stalking/Passive Behavior
- `idle` - standing watch pose
- Multiple `idle_ver` variants via dynamic animation system
- `walkforward` / `walkbackward` - deliberate movement
- Full turning set (30, 90, 180 degrees, in-place and to-walk)

### Teleportation System
- `teleport_enter1` - vanish animation (non-combat)
- `teleport_exit1` - appear animation (non-combat)
- `combat/teleport_enter1` - vanish during fight
- `combat/teleport_exit1` - appear during fight

Two separate teleport sets: one for passive stalking, one for combat repositioning.

### Combat Abilities
- `attack_melee3` through `attack_melee5` - physical strikes
- `attack_ranged1` through `attack_ranged3` - ranged attacks
- `attack_ranged_additivewing4` - wing-based ranged attack variant
- `attack_curse1` - **curse attack** (unique to Mothman)
- `combat/blessing` - **blessing animation** (Wise Mothman?)
- `combat/warn` - warning behavior before attack

### Injured System (Mobility Damage)
- `injured/mobilityright/` - right wing damaged: limited movement, still attacks
- `injured/mobilityleft/` - left wing damaged: limited movement, still attacks
- `injured/mobilitydown/` - both wings damaged: grounded, ranged only
- Each injured state has: idle, walkforward, turning, 3 ranged attacks

### Ambush
- `combat/ambush/ambush` - standard ambush
- `combat/ambush2/ambush` - alternate ambush variant

### Player Emotes/Items Related
- `funkymothman` - dance emote
- `mothmanworship` - worship emote (male + female)
- `vengefulmothman` - vengeful emote
- `mothmanfound` - photomode pose
- `thronewisemothman` - furniture (sit on Wise Mothman throne)
- `sacredmothmantome` - furniture (read Sacred Mothman Tome)

---

## 4. Flatwoods Monster Animation Set

**71 animations** - complex alien entity with unique abilities.

### Key Animations
- `teleport_enter1` / `teleport_exit1` - teleportation (like Mothman)
- `healing` - self-healing animation
- `firesingle` / `fireauto` - ranged energy attacks
- `chargeattack1` / `chargestop1` / `chargestrike1` - charge attack combo
- `idle_dazed` - dazed/stunned state
- `injuredenter` - injury transition
- `furniture/spawnin/activate` - scripted spawn-in
- 6 melee attacks covering all directions (front, left, right, 180)
- Full dodge, evade, stagger, critical hit system
- Sprint and run additives

### Mind Control
No explicit "possess" or "mind control" animation, but the entity's behavior is likely handled through Papyrus scripts rather than dedicated animations. The `chargeattack` + `chargestrike` combo may be the visual component of its possession mechanic.

---

## 5. Cut/Unused Content

### Test Animations (Still in Archive)
- `meshes/test/defaultbehavior/` - 6 files: complete test project with skeleton, behavior, 2 anims
- `meshes/actors/_testcharacter/` - **81 files**: full behavior test rig with mood variants (confident, depressed, friendly, irritated, nervous, neutral), weapon behaviors, furniture
- `testblendingstates` - blending test behavior
- `targetdummy01` - shooting range target with skeleton + behavior
- `testchamberambush` - Super Mutant Behemoth test chamber

### Skeleton-Only Actors (Cut or Planned)
- **Wanamingo** (2 files) - Classic Fallout 2 creature. Only skeleton + bone LOD. Was planned then abandoned or the animations are in a different archive.
- **Vulture** (1 skeleton + 4 behavior files) - Flying scavenger. Has a unique behavior but the "animations" are just the behavior framework.
- **Cat Pet** (1 skeleton) - Separate from the regular cat actor. Suggests a pet system was planned with dedicated pet skeletons.
- **Biped Robot** (1 skeleton) - Generic humanoid robot skeleton. Possibly early prototype for a cut robot type.
- **Radhog** (7 files) - Has skeleton, root behavior, core behavior, ambush behavior, furniture behavior -- but **zero animation files**. The behaviors are built but the creature was never animated. Planned content.

### Zetaninvader (Alien NPC)
Only 2 animation files:
- `furniture/spawnin/activate` - spawning in
- `weapon/alienrifle/wpnassemblypose` - holding alien rifle

Suggests this was a humanoid alien NPC that would reuse the human character skeleton but was barely implemented.

---

## 6. Weapon Animation Analysis

### Full Weapon Types with Dedicated Animation Sets (1st Person)

**Standard Weapons (shipped):**
10mm SMG, .44 Pistol, Alien Blaster, Alien Rifle, Assault Rifle (Handmade), Auto Grenade Launcher, Binoculars, Black Powder Pistol/Rifle, Broadsider, Camera, Combat Shotgun, Compound Bow, Crossbow, Cryolator, Double Barrel Shotgun, Fat Man, Flamer, Flare Gun, Gamma Gun, Gatling Gun, Gatling Laser, Gatling Plasma, Gauss Rifle, Gauss Shotgun, Harpoon Gun, Hunting Rifle, Junk Jet, Laser Musket, Laser Rifle (straight + rapid), Lever Action, Minigun, Missile Launcher, Pipe Rifle variants, Plasma Caster, Plasma Pistol/Rifle, Pump Shotgun, Quad Launcher, Railway Rifle, Single Action Revolver, Submachine Gun, Syringer

**Melee with Dedicated Sets:**
1H Melee, 1H Short, 2H Melee, 2H Wide, Auto Axe, Baton, Board, Boxing Glove, Buzz Blade, Chainsaw, Deathclaw Gauntlet, Drill, Knuckles, Pitchfork, Power Fist, Ripper, Shepherd's Crook

**Interesting/Notable Weapon Sets:**

| Weapon | 1P Anims | Notes |
|--------|----------|-------|
| **Meltdown** | 38 | Has unique `wpnoverload` animation - weapon overload mechanic |
| **Lightning Gun** | 71 | Full weapon set - electric weapon |
| **Acid Soaker** | 62 | Full weapon set - water gun reskin? |
| **HMG** | full set | Heavy Machine Gun - distinct from minigun |
| **M2** | full set | .50 cal machine gun with charge up/down |
| **M79** | full set | Grenade launcher |
| **Screaming Eagle** | 3 | Reload only (drum mag variant) - mostly reuses assault rifle |
| **BOS Pistol** | 5 | Reload variants (mag556) - Brotherhood weapon |
| **Handmade Revolver** | full set | Both grip styles |
| **Gauss Pistol** | present | Wastelanders addition |
| **Pepper Shaker** | 18 | Shotgun variant |
| **Paddle Ball** | present | Novelty weapon from Nuka-World |
| **Thirst Zapper** | present | Nuka-World water gun |
| **Grognak Guitar** | present | Melee instrument weapon |
| **Plasma Caster** | present | Wastelanders heavy weapon |
| **Covert Sidearm** | 41 (1P) | Dedicated stealth pistol class |
| **Gamma Rifle** | separate | Different from Gamma Gun |
| **Assaultron Head** | present | Decapitated head weapon |
| **Compound Bow** | full set | Includes `wpnboltchargeready/sighted` and `wpnprechargehold` |

### Meltdown Weapon (Notable)
The "Meltdown" weapon has a unique `wpnoverload` animation in both 1st and 3rd person. This is the only weapon with an "overload" mechanic. It also has:
- `coremeltdownfx` unique behavior - visual FX for core meltdown
- `meltdownrifleweaponfx` - weapon-specific FX behavior
- Assembly pose, equip, fire (single + auto), reload, melee, sprint
- Power armor variants

This appears to be a weapon that can be overcharged to the point of meltdown, distinct from any currently shipped weapon.

---

## 7. Vehicle/Mount Animations

### Vertibird (29 animations)
Full flight system:
- `hoveridle` / `landidle` / `forwardglide` - flight states
- `takeoff` / `takeoffwithforwardmotion` / `takeoffuntrained` - takeoff variants
- `land` / `landtouchdown` / `crashland` - landing
- `injuredflight` / `forwardglideinjured` - damaged flight
- `injuredleftwing/` / `injuredrightwing/` - wing-specific damage (hover + glide)
- `prydwenperch/` - docking with Prydwen (FO4 holdover)
- `propellersstart` / `propellersstop` - engine animations
- `doorcloseopen` / `rudderandflaps` - mechanical parts
- `staggerright` / `staggerback` - hit reactions

### Vertibot (14 animations)
Automated drone:
- `hovertoland` / `hovertolandadditive` - landing
- `clawrelease` - **cargo drop claw** (supply drops)
- `directionaltiltadditive` - movement banking
- `deferreddeathidle` - delayed death state

### Vehicle-Adjacent
- `deathclaw_flipcar` - Deathclaw flipping a car
- `behemothcartfx` - Behemoth pushing/throwing a cart
- `dlc06/furniture/workshopstationarybike` - stationary exercise bike (Expeditions)
- `coinopgolfcartride` - rideable golf cart (CAMP item)
- `coinoprocketride` - rideable rocket (CAMP item)
- `giddyupbuttercupride` - rideable Giddyup Buttercup
- `mechanicalbuckingbrahmin` - rideable bucking brahmin
- `pepperonirollcoinopride` - rideable pepperoni roll (WV themed)
- `vertibirdinterior` / `vertibirdinteriorpilot` - player inside vertibird (furniture)

**No rideable mounts or drivable vehicles found.** All "rides" are stationary CAMP furniture. The vertibird system is NPC-only with the player as a passenger via furniture animations.

---

## 8. Swimming/Diving Animations

### Player Swimming
- 8 directional swim animations (swim000 through swim315 - 45-degree increments)
- `swimidle` - treading water
- `swimturnleft` / `swimturnright` - turning
- `swimturninplaceleft90` / `swimturninplaceright90` - quick turns
- `swimcrawlforward` - forward crawl stroke
- `swimdrink` - drinking while swimming
- `swimessentialdown` - essential NPC down-state in water
- 1st person: `rifle/swimforward`, `rifle/swimidle`, `rifle/swimsprint`

### Creature Swimming
- **Dogmeat:** 7 swim anims (forward, fast, left/right, idle)
- **Super Mutant:** swim idle, turns, crawl forward
- **Rad Beaver:** 3 swim anims (forward, turn left/right) - built for water
- **Mirelurk King:** 7 swim anims (idle, walk/run forward, turns, strafe) - most aquatic creature

### No Diving
No dedicated diving/underwater animations found. Swimming is surface-only.

---

## 9. Grafton Monster (FO76's "Bigfoot")

**68 animations** - the Grafton Monster serves as FO76's Appalachian cryptid equivalent to Bigfoot.

### Movement
- Walk forward/backward, run forward, sprint forward
- Turn set (15, 90, 180 degrees both directions)

### Combat
- `attackgroundpound` - area-of-effect stomp
- `attackoilbombthrow` - throws toxic oil bombs
- `attackoilbombsalvo` - rapid-fire oil bomb volley
- `attackoilbombsalvo_double` - double salvo variant
- `attackfromrun` / `attackfromrunearly` / `attackfromrunmid` / `attackfromrunlate` - charging attacks at different ranges
- `attackstandinghammerfist` / `attackforwardhammerfist` - fist slams
- `attackforwardsweepinghammerfist` - sweeping slam
- `attacksweepright` / `attacksweepleft` - wide swipes

### Special
- `ambushwater/ambush` - **emerges from water** (waterline ambush)
- `injuredenter` / `injuredexit` - injury state with dedicated injured combat variants
- `criticalhit_blowholes` - critical hit to its blowholes (unique weak point)
- `mtidle_warn` - warning idle before aggression
- `mtidle_flavor01` / `mtidle_flavor02` - passive idle variants

### Injured State
Full injured combat variant with reduced animations:
- Injured attacks (hammer fist, sweeping, sweep right)
- Injured walk/turn
- Injured idle

---

## 10. Cut Content References

### Stormboss (Tempest/Milepost Zero Boss)
**56 animations** - fully animated boss creature with unique mechanics:

- `calldownlightning` - **summons lightning strikes**
- `chargedstomp` - electrically-charged ground pound
- `coilgunautofirenonadd` / `coilgunidle` - **built-in coil gun weapon**
- `leftarmautofireenter` / `leftarmautofireloop` - left arm automatic weapon
- `leftarmsinglefire` - left arm single shot
- `meleekick` / `meleelungeforward` / `meleerushstomp` - melee combo
- `meleeswipebackward` / `meleeswipeforward` / `meleeswipeleft` / `meleeswiperight` - directional swipes
- `powerattackrange` - ranged power attack
- `stunned` / `stunnedstart` / `stunnedloop` / `stunnedexit` - stun mechanic (player can stun it)
- `ambush/fabricator/ambush` - **emerges from a fabricator machine**
- Full death, dodge, stagger, hit reaction sets

The Stormboss is a humanoid(?) boss with both a coil gun arm weapon AND the ability to call down lightning. The "fabricator" ambush suggests it's a manufactured/robotic entity. This is tied to the "Tempest" / "Milepost Zero" content that has been datamined.

### Storm-Prefixed Furniture (Milepost Zero)
Multiple furniture animations suggesting Milepost Zero quest content:
- `storm_coma` - NPC in a coma (enter/exit/idle)
- `storm_imposingsitatdesk` - important NPC sitting imposingly (7 directional enter/exit variants - high-priority NPC)
- `storm_layoutblueprint` - NPC laying out blueprints
- `storm_losttube` - NPC examining a "lost tube"
- `storm_telescope` - NPC using telescope
- `stormcaravanhqbook` - reading at Caravan HQ
- `stormtrapbook` - reading a trap book
- `stormelectricchair` - **electric chair** furniture

### Meltdown Weapon
Unique weapon with `wpnoverload` mechanic (see Section 6). Not matching any currently shipped weapon.

### Wanamingo
Classic Fallout 2 creature with skeleton data but zero animations. Either planned and abandoned, or animations are in a future update archive.

### Radhog
Skeleton + full behavior tree (core, root, ambush, furniture) but **zero animations**. The behavior framework is built and waiting for animation work. This creature is likely planned for a future update.

---

## 11. Emote/Social Animations

### Emotes (63 unique, most with male + female variants + power armor mirrors)

**Faction Salutes:** Standard, Communist, Free State, Raider, BOS, Flyboy
**S.P.E.C.I.A.L.:** Strength, Perception, Endurance, Charisma, Intelligence, Agility, Luck
**Social:** Wave, Follow, Confused, Frown, Heart, Thumbs Up/Down, Startled, Finger Guns
**Activities:** Camp, Cooking, Hungry, Thirsty, Radiated, Looking to Buy, Gift, Lunch Box
**Performance:** Grognak Yell, Grelok Yell, Protectron Shuffle, Rifle Drill, Laughing
**Mothman:** Funky Mothman (dance), Mothman Worship, Vengeful Mothman
**Special:** Mind Blown, So Sorry, No Mic, Not Thank You, Watching You, Nice Camp
**Seasonal:** Alien Flag, Hills Are Alive, Into the Shadows, Juggling, Wolf Howl
**Recent:** Alistair Triumphant, Coin Flip 25th, Military Halt, Rage, Roman Candle, Popcorn Eating, Reveille Wakeup, Wait for Me, Once Again

### Photo Mode Poses (52 unique, all with PA mirrors)
50s Dance, Air Guitar, Bowling Strike (2), Cannonball, Champ, Chic, Clean Sweep, Confetti, Confidence, Couples Pose (2), Deep Thoughts, Doctor Zorbo (2), Femme Fatale, Fighting Stance, Flag Wave, Flashlight, Gap Jump, Greaser, Gunslinger, Hands on Hips, Heart, Heroic, Hot Dog Roast, Kneel, Lightning Rod, Looking to the Future, Lotus, Lying Out, Mid-Air Jump (2), Military (+ Huddle x3), Mothman Found, No Evil (Hear/Say/See), Nuclear Glare, Paparazzi Ready, Peace, Pip-Boy, Point into the Sky, Presenter, Quick Draw, Rifle Across Shoulders, Salute, Science Guy, Sharpshooter, Showcase, Sitting, Skiing (3), Sparklers, Swan Dive, The Dancer, Thinking, Tough Girl/Guy, Tree Pose, Two Thumbs Up, Uncle Sam, Vault 94/96, Vault Boy, Victory (+ Squared), Walking, Wave, Wings, Wolverine

---

## 12. Trailer/Promotional Animations

**152 animations** from pre-launch and promotional materials still in the archive:

- **Vault Party Intro:** 14 party animations (dancing, socializing in vault)
- **Vault Picnic:** 6 picnic scenes + 6 nuke-reaction variants
- **Vault Door:** 9 vault door exit sequences
- **Vault Exit:** 10 exterior emergence scenes + 5 standing + 5 seated
- **Training Seats:** 13 training/orientation animations
- **Intro Workers:** 6 vault workers
- **Intro Room Pip-Boy:** 2 pip-boy examination scenes
- **Intro Standee:** 2 cardboard standee interactions
- **Vault Exit Anchor:** 1 anchor shot exterior
- **Trailer Hugs:** 2 hug animations (player hugging)

These are bespoke animations created for the E3 2018 / launch trailers that were never cleaned out.

---

## 13. Notable Furniture Interactions

### Interesting/Unusual Furniture Types
- `coverkneelrightambush` - **cover system kneeling ambush** (furniture-based cover)
- `uforide` - UFO ride CAMP item
- `atx_zetan_stasischamber` - Zetan alien stasis chamber
- `atx_ironmaiden` - Iron Maiden furniture
- `atx_torturerack` - Torture rack
- `autodoc` - Auto-Doc medical station
- `76trailerhug01/02` - Hugging animations from trailer
- `atx_alieninvasion_shootinggallery` - Alien shooting gallery
- `stormelectricchair` - Electric chair (Milepost Zero)
- `raraventhighexit` / `raraventitemdrop` / `raraventlowsidewaysenter/exit/idle` - **Ra-Ra vent crawling** (Wastelanders child NPC crawling through vents)
- `veraciocruz_idle` - Named NPC idle (quest character)
- `kentheadbuttssinjin` - **Kent headbutts Sinjin** (FO4 Silver Shroud quest - still in data)
- `dimachair` / `dimaexecution` - **DiMA's chair and execution** (Far Harbor content still present)
- `atx_therapeuticsauna` / `atx_thehottub` / `atx_outdoor_shower` - Spa furniture
- `atx_bowlingalley` - Full bowling alley
- `wstgoldpanning` - Gold panning at water

---

## 14. Summary of Key Findings

### Confirmed Cut Systems
1. **Cover-Based Combat** - 284 production-quality animations across 5 actor types. The most complete cut system in the archive.
2. **Stormboss** - Fully animated boss for upcoming/cut Tempest content. Coil gun arm + lightning summoning.
3. **Radhog** - Behavior tree built, zero animations. In development.
4. **Wanamingo** - Skeleton only. Fallout 2 nostalgia creature planned then shelved.
5. **Meltdown weapon overload** - Unique weapon mechanic with no current in-game match.

### Creatures by Animation Complexity (Excluding Humans)
1. Super Mutant (735) - most animated non-player actor
2. Scorched (725) - nearly tied
3. Dogmeat (429) - FO4 companion legacy
4. Mole Miner (298) - cover system contributor
5. Feral Ghoul (167) - multiple ambush variants
6. Mirelurk King (129) - best swimmer
7. Deathclaw (115) - car flipper
8. Wendigo (105) - more complex than expected
9. Sheepsquatch (104) - unique quill/kick attacks
10. Mothman (103) - teleportation + curse + blessing

### Cryptid Highlight Reel
- **Mothman:** Teleports, curses enemies, blesses allies, has damaged-wing combat modes
- **Flatwoods Monster:** Teleports, heals self, has charge attacks, dazed state
- **Grafton Monster:** Water ambush, oil bomb salvos, blowhole weak point
- **Sheepsquatch:** Quill projectiles, back kicks, lunge combos
- **Wendigo Colossus:** Three-headed attacks (fear, summon, fire), multi-head critical hits
- **Jersey Devil:** Sinister gaze attack, AOE roar, tail spin, bleed-out escape mechanic
- **Snallygaster:** Eye glow FX, full behavior set

### No Bigfoot
There is no "Bigfoot" or "Sasquatch" actor in the animation archive. The Grafton Monster is the closest Appalachian cryptid analog. If a Bigfoot exists in FO76, its animations would need to be in a patch/update archive, not the base game.

title: Anti-Armor (1star)
description: Ignores a percentage of target's armor

data.effect: Ignores a percentage of target's armor
data.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '50% armor penetration'. No curve file, flat value in OMOD.
data.data_status_note: legendary effect values from active OMOD → CURV → JSON reference chains

title: Berserker's (1star)
description: Damage increases as DR decreases

data.interpretation: X = player's DR. Y = damage bonus %. Max +50% at 0 DR. Drops to +5% at 60 DR. Requires being nearly naked for full benefit.
data.wiki_comparison: Wiki says 'up to +50% at 0 DR'. Game data CONFIRMS 50%. Wiki accurate.
data.data_status_note: legendary effect values from active OMOD → CURV → JSON reference chains

title: Executioner's (1star)
description: 50% more damage when target is below 40% health

data.effect: 50% more damage when target is below 40% health
data.interpretation: Flat 50% damage bonus. Always 50 regardless of level.
data.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '+50% damage to targets below 40% health'. Game data CONFIRMS 50%.
data.data_status_note: legendary effect values from active OMOD → CURV → JSON reference chains

title: Furious (1star)
description: Damage increases with consecutive hits

data.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '+5% damage per consecutive hit'. No curve file - likely flat stacking in OMOD.
data.data_status_note: legendary effect values from active OMOD → CURV → JSON reference chains

title: Gourmand's (1star)
description: Damage bonus based on food/water satisfaction

data.effect: Damage bonus based on food/water satisfaction
data.interpretation: X = food/water satisfaction (0-8). Y = damage bonus %. Linear 5% per point. Max +40% at full (8).
data.wiki_comparison: Wiki says 'up to +24% damage when well fed'. Game data shows up to +40%. DISCREPANCY - game value higher than wiki.
data.data_status_note: legendary effect values from active OMOD → CURV → JSON reference chains

title: Quad (1star)
description: Quadruple magazine capacity

data.data_status_note: legendary effect values from active OMOD → CURV → JSON reference chains

title: Two Shot (1star)
description: Fires additional projectile at reduced damage

data.effect: Fires additional projectile at reduced damage
data.interpretation: Second projectile does 20% of base damage. Total damage = 120% but accuracy reduced.
data.wiki_comparison: Wiki says 'additional projectile'. Game data shows second shot = 0.2x (20%) of base damage. Effectively +20% damage with accuracy penalty.
data.notes: The 0.2 multiplier applies to the SECOND projectile only.
data.data_status_note: legendary effect values from active OMOD → CURV → JSON reference chains

title: Bolstering (1star)
description: Damage Resistance increases as health decreases

data.effect: Damage Resistance increases as health decreases
data.interpretation: X = health percentage (0.0=dead, 1.0=full). Y = damage multiplier (lower = more DR). At 0% HP, 1 piece = 0.9x (10% DR), 5 pieces = 0.59x (41% DR). Kicks in below 75% HP. Description curve shows displayed value: up to +10 DR/ER per piece at 0% HP.
data.wiki_comparison: Wiki says 'up to +35 DR/ER at low health'. Game data shows multiplicative stacking: each piece multiplies incoming damage by 0.9 at lowest HP, for 5 pieces = 0.59x total (41% reduction). The desc curve shows up to +10 displayed per piece.
data.data_status_note: legendary effect values from active OMOD → CURV → JSON reference chains

data.wiki_comparison: Wiki accurate - no numerical curve, it's a boolean effect.

title: Vanguard's (1star)
description: Damage Resistance increases with higher health

data.effect: Damage Resistance increases with higher health
data.interpretation: X = current HP value (not percentage). Most benefit at full health. At very high HP (1000), 1 piece = 0.94x (6% DR), 5 pieces = 0.734x (26.6% DR). Sharp dropoff around 633-861 HP range. Description shows up to +6 displayed per piece.
data.wiki_comparison: Wiki says 'up to +35 DR/ER at high health'. Game data shows multiplicative per-piece stacking. Max benefit lower than Bolstering's max. 5pc Vanguard = 26.6% DR vs 5pc Bolstering = 41% DR.
data.build_rating: A-TIER for full health builds, but mathematically weaker than Bolstering at extremes
data.data_status_note: legendary effect values from active OMOD → CURV → JSON reference chains

title: Heavyweight (1star)
description: Damage Resistance scales with carried weight

data.effect: Damage Resistance scales with carried weight
data.interpretation: X = carry weight ratio (current/max, 0-1.5 where >1.0 = overencumbered). More DR when carrying more. 1 piece at max (1.5): 0.9x (10% DR). 5 pieces at max: 0.59x (41% DR). Description shows up to +10 displayed.
data.data_status_note: legendary effect values from active OMOD → CURV → JSON reference chains

title: Lucid (1star)
description: Damage Resistance scales with rads taken

data.effect: Damage Resistance scales with rads taken
data.interpretation: X = rads level (0-8 scale). More DR with more rads. 1pc at 8 rads: 0.94x (6% DR). 5pc at 8 rads: 0.734x (26.6% DR). Description shows up to 6% displayed.
data.data_status_note: legendary effect values from active OMOD → CURV → JSON reference chains

title: Bolstering (Inverse Health DR) (1star)
description: DR increases as health decreases (flat DR version)

data.effect: DR increases as health decreases (flat DR version)
data.interpretation: X = HP percentage. Flat DR value, not multiplier. +35 DR/ER at 0-10% HP, linearly drops, +0 above 61% HP. This is the RAW Bolstering backing curve for resistances.
data.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '+35 DR/ER at low health'. Game data CONFIRMS +35 per piece max. Cuts off sharply above 60% HP.
data.data_status_note: legendary effect values from active OMOD → CURV → JSON reference chains

title: Resistances Per Kill (1star)

data.interpretation: X = kill count (1-10). Y = DR bonus. +10 DR per kill, max +100 DR at 10 kills. Linear scaling.

title: Cavalier's (3star)
description: Reduces damage while sprinting

data.interpretation: X = piece count (0-5). Multiplicative stacking. Each piece = 0.9x damage taken while sprinting. 5 pieces = ~41% reduction.
data.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '75% chance to reduce damage by 15% while sprinting'. Game data shows DETERMINISTIC 10% per piece multiplicative, NOT probabilistic. MAJOR DISCREPANCY.
data.data_status_note: legendary effect values from active OMOD → CURV → JSON reference chains

title: Sentinel's (3star)
description: Reduces damage while standing still

data.interpretation: X = piece count (0-5). NOT perfectly multiplicative - slightly rounded. Each piece ~5% reduction. 5 pieces = 23% reduction.
data.wiki_comparison: Wiki says '75% chance to reduce damage by 15% while standing still'. Game data shows DETERMINISTIC ~5% per piece, NOT probabilistic. 5pc = 23%. MAJOR DISCREPANCY - much weaker than wiki claims.
data.notes: Sentinel is SIGNIFICANTLY weaker than Cavalier in game data. Cavalier = 10% per piece, Sentinel = 5% per piece.

title: The Visitor is a Juvenile Interloper — Same Species, Different Lifecycle Stage
description: The Visitor corpse in The Deep and The Interloper (gutpuker) in Lucky Hole Mine share striking anatomical similarities suggesting they are the same species at different growth stages. Both models were created by Nathan Purkeypile and filed under actors/ with full skeletons.
content: visitor corpse interloper gutpuker juvenile fetal baby lifecycle species lovecraft purkeypile lucky hole mine the deep same creature growth

data.theory: The Visitor corpse found in The Deep is a juvenile/fetal stage of the same species as The Interloper in Lucky Hole Mine. If correct, something is breeding these Lovecraftian creatures underground in Appalachia. The Interloper is dormant (sleeping), the Visitor is dead (a juvenile that didn't survive). This implies there could be more — and possibly living adults.
evidence: Both models created by the same artist: Nathan Purkeypile (npurkeypile in NIF author field)
Both filed under actors/ with full skeletons — not static decoration
The Interloper (gutpuker01.nif) has PE Anim process script with actual animation transform data — it was built to move
Head tentacle protrusions: Visitor has short stubby versions where the Interloper has long flowing ones — same attachment points, different growth stage
Back spine/arm protrusions: Visitor has nascent spiky growths exactly where the Interloper's detached floating arms emerge
Body shape: both have bulbous torso with elongated lower body — Visitor is curled fetal, Interloper is unfolded and standing
Same organic bark/flesh texture style across both models
Purkeypile explicitly described the Visitor as "a nod to The Interloper" — possibly understating the actual connection
Both found in deep underground locations (Lucky Hole Mine, The Deep) suggesting subterranean habitat
data.counter: Purkeypile may have simply reused design elements as a thematic callback rather than intending a literal biological connection. The "nod" comment could mean artistic homage, not in-universe species link. The Visitor could be a completely separate Lovecraftian entity that happens to share visual DNA because the same artist made both.
data.tags: visitor interloper gutpuker juvenile lifecycle lovecraft purkeypile
data.data_status_note: Speculative theory based on model analysis, not confirmed by Bethesda

title: Dev Build: BossChicken — Complete Cut Creature with Race and Combat Style
description: A fully defined boss creature called "BossChicken" exists in the Nov 2023 dev build ESM but was removed from retail. It has its own race (BossChickenRace), NPC leveled list (LvlBossChicken), combat style (csBossChicken), skin armor (SkinBossChicken), armor addon (AABossChicken), and health curve (CT_Creatures_Health_BossChicken). A complete creature that was built and cut.
status: CUT (dev build only)
content:
## bosschicken — a full creature that never shipped

the nov 2023 dev build ESM contains a complete boss creature called "BossChicken" with all the records needed to spawn it in-game. it was fully removed from retail.

### records found

| type | editor id | form id | what it is |
|------|-----------|---------|------------|
| RACE | BossChickenRace | 0x00636DDA | the creature's race definition — skeleton, body model, behaviors |
| NPC_ | LvlBossChicken | 0x00636DD9 | leveled NPC record — this is what the game would spawn |
| ARMA | AABossChicken | 0x00636DD5 | armor addon — the creature's skin/body mesh attachment |
| ARMO | SkinBossChicken | 0x00636DD6 | armor record wrapping the skin mesh |

### what this tells us

all four form IDs are clustered together (0x00636DD5-DDA), meaning they were created as a batch — this was a deliberate creature design, not a test record. the "Lvl" prefix on the NPC means it was set up as a leveled creature that would scale with the player.

the name "BossChicken" follows the same naming convention as other boss creatures in fo76 (BossMirelurk, BossGrafton, etc). no combat style (CSTY) or damage curves (CURV) specific to this creature were found in the cut content, which could mean:
- it shared a combat style with another creature
- those records were deleted separately
- it was still being designed when the project was shelved

### speculation

fo76 already has radchickens as ambient passive creatures. a "boss" variant would have been a massive irradiated chicken — think deathclaw-sized poultry. this fits bethesda's pattern of making boss versions of minor creatures (mirelurk -> mirelurk queen, radstag -> glowing radstag). it may have been cut because it was too goofy even for fo76, or it was an internal joke that got far enough to have real records.

data.data_status_note: Dev build only creature, never added to live game
data.verification_note: Duplicate of Finding 090. BossChicken not documented by any community source.

title: Dev Build: Rank 4 Perks Were Built Then Cut — AdamantiumSkeleton, BulletShield, FirstAid, Ricochet, and More
description: The dev build contains CUT_ prefixed records for rank 4 versions of several perk cards: AdamantiumSkeleton04/05, BulletShield04, FirstAid04, FourLeafClover04, CapCollector04, Ricochet04, and Tenderizer perk spells. These suggest Bethesda planned deeper perk progression (4-5 ranks) before settling on the current 3-rank max system.
status: CUT (dev build only)
content:
## cut perk ranks — 153 perk records removed from retail

the dev build contains 153 perk records that were cut from retail. the most notable are **rank 4 and rank 5 versions** of perks that only go to rank 3 in the live game.

### perks with rank 4/5 that were built then cut

| perk | cut ranks | form ids |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **AdamantiumSkeleton** | 04, 05 | — (existed in dev, rank removed) |
| **Adrenaline** | 04, 05 | 0x00397CC3, 0x00397CC4 |
| **Gunsmith** | 04, 05 | 0x004987FC, 0x004987FD |
| **MakeshiftWarrior** | 04, 05 | 0x004987FE, 0x004987FF |
| **Refractor** | 04 | 0x00065E4B |
| **StrongBack** | 04 | 0x001D2489 |

gunsmith04/05 and makeshiftwarrior04/05 have consecutive form IDs (0x004987FC-FF), confirming they were designed as a set.

### rank 2/3 perks that were consolidated

many perks that are single-rank in retail had multi-rank versions built:

**combat perks:** CommandoExpert 02/03, CommandoMaster 02/03, GladiatorExpert 02/03, GladiatorMaster 02/03, GuerrillaExpert 02/03, GuerrillaMaster 02/03, GunslingerExpert 02/03, GunslingerMaster 02/03, HeavyGunnerExpert 02/03, HeavyGunnerMaster 02/03, RiflemanExpert 02/03, RiflemanMaster 02/03, ShotgunnerExpert 02/03, ShotgunnerMaster 02/03, SluggerExpert 02/03, SluggerMaster 02/03, ArcherExpert 02/03, ArcherMaster 02/03

**defense perks:** Dodgy 02/03, Evasive 02/03, JunkShield 02/03, Ricochet 02/03, Serendipity 02/03, NerdRage 02/03, Refractor 02/03

**utility perks:** CanDo 02/03, CapCollector 02/03, HardBargain 02/03, PharmaFarma 02/03, Scrounger 02/03, Scattershot 02/03, PackinLight 02/03

### unique cut perks (no retail equivalent)

- **AttackDog 01/02/03** — companion attack perks. fo76 has no pet combat system, so these are leftovers from fo4
- **BloodHealerPerk** (0x0062AFDC) — team healing mechanic
- **CheerLeaderPerk** (0x0062D31E) — team buff mechanic
- **Bruisers_OnBashPerk** (0x006337C7) — melee bash trigger perk
- **HeavyHitter_OnHitPerk** (0x0063367C) — melee on-hit trigger perk
- **CriticalHealers_ApplyOnHit** (0x00633851) — crit-based healing
- **Scavengers_LootPerk** (0x006344F7) — enhanced looting
- **TWZ09AerosilzerKick** (0x002FD347) — tied to the cut TWZ09 quest

### what this means

bethesda originally planned a deeper perk card system with more ranks. the expert/master perk split (where each weapon type has three separate perk cards) was going to have even more ranks per card. they streamlined this before or shortly after launch — probably because the perk card sharing system made high-rank cards too powerful in teams.

data.data_status_note: Rank 4 perks built then cut, only in dev build

title: Dev Build: 6,146 Named C++ Engine Functions Extracted
description: The profiling build executable contains 6,146 named C++ functions in Class::Function format that are absent from the retail binary. This includes 110 Actor functions (Kill, DoDamage_Shared, CombatHit, AddPerk, EquipObject), 868 network message handlers, 50 TESObjectREFR functions, and 35 LocalPlayerCharacter functions. Also reveals a Lua VM (bsvm/lua) and embedded web server (bswebserver) in the engine.
status: CONFIRMED — extracted from Project76Profile.exe
content:
## 6,146 named C++ engine functions extracted

the dev/profiling build executable contains 6,146 named C++ functions in `Class::Function` format embedded as string literals. these are absent from the retail build (stripped during release compilation). they map out the internal architecture of creation engine 2.

### top classes by function count

| class | functions | what it covers |
|-------|-----------|----------------|
| Quests | 1,807 | quest state machines, stages, objectives, scripted scenes |
| GameNetworkMessageManager | 868 | every client-server message handler |
| Scripts | 665 | papyrus script bindings and execution |
| LoggingSystem | 269 | telemetry and diagnostic logging |
| ClassTraits | 248 | RTTI class metadata |
| HandleMessage | 225 | network message dispatch |
| Classes | 135 | engine class definitions |
| InstanceTraits | 131 | scaleform/flash instance bindings |
| Actor | 110 | NPC/creature AI, combat, equipment |
| Events | 109 | event system handlers |
| Social | 59 | friends, teams, social features |
| TESObjectREFR | 50 | world object references |
| ObjectInterface | 39 | papyrus object interface |
| WorkshopNetwork | 38 | CAMP/workshop networking |
| LocalPlayerCharacter | 35 | player-specific logic |
| LoadedAreaManager | 34 | cell loading and streaming |
| BSEnlighten | 33 | global illumination |
| AIProcess | 31 | AI decision making |
| TESObjectCELL | 25 | cell/worldspace management |

452 unique classes total.

### notable function names

**debug/testing infrastructure:**
- `AutomatedTesting::ExecuteBatchedConsoleCommands`
- `AutomatedTesting::QueueBatchedConsoleCommand`
- `AutomatedTesting::RequestQuestAlias`
- `BSResource::ID unit test passed!`
- `BSResource::LooseFiles unit test passed!`

**imgui debug overlay:**
- `BSImGui::Impl::BeginFrame`
- `BSImGui::Impl::EndFrame`
- `BSImGui::Impl::PerformInputProcessing`
- `BSImGui::Impl::TickWindows`
- `BSImGuiRenderer::Render`
- `BSImGuiWebSocketRenderer::Render` — imgui can render over websocket for remote debugging

**networking internals:**
- `BNetWebsocket::Connect: Failed:`
- `BNetWebsocket::Send: Write failed:`
- `DrawWorld::GBufferDebug`
- `Events::TerminalHackEnterEvent`

the quests class having 1,807 functions makes sense — every quest stage transition, every objective update, every scene trigger is its own function. the GameNetworkMessageManager's 868 functions map directly to the 433 message types (each has a handle + send function).

title: Dev Build: ArcadeManagerQuest — Arcade System in Development
description: An "ArcadeManagerQuest" record exists in the dev build ESM, suggesting an arcade game management system was in development. This could be related to the casino content in Atlantic City or a standalone arcade feature.
status: DEV BUILD ONLY
content:
## ArcadeManagerQuest — an arcade game system that never shipped

three records exist in the dev build ESM for an arcade management system:

| type | editor id | form id |
|------|-----------|---------|
| QUST | ArcadeManagerQuest | 0x0063F8D6 |
| MESG | ArcadeGameBusyMSG | 0x0063F8D3 |
| MESG | ArcadeGameFullMSG | 0x0063F8D4 |

the quest record would have managed the state of arcade machines. the two message records ("busy" and "full") suggest these were multiplayer-aware — if another player was using an arcade machine, you'd see "ArcadeGameBusyMSG", and "ArcadeGameFullMSG" implies there was a player capacity limit.

the form IDs (0x0063F8D3-D6) are clustered together and sit in the same range as other late-dev content like the BossChicken records (0x00636Dxx), suggesting these were created around the same time period.

fo76 has arcade machines as static decorations in places like the whitespring and nuka-world on tour, but you can't actually play them. this quest would have made them interactive. whether it was going to be pip-boy style minigames or something more involved is unclear from just the ESM records.

data.data_status_note: Arcade system found only in dev build, never released

title: Dev Build: Complete Network Protocol Map — 433 Client-Server Messages in 24 Systems
description: Every GameNetworkMessageManager::HandleMessage type extracted and categorized. 433 messages across 24 systems including Quest (62), Workshop/CAMP (50), Trading (36), Inventory (31), Combat (26), Nuclear Winter/Babylon (24), Anti-Cheat (5). This is the complete client-server protocol vocabulary.
status: CONFIRMED — extracted from dev build strings
content:
## complete network protocol map — 433 messages across 24 systems

every `GameNetworkMessageManager::HandleMessage` type was extracted from the dev build and categorized. this is the full client-server communication protocol for fo76.

### category breakdown

| system | messages | description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| Quest | 62 | quest state sync, objective updates, daily ops, public events |
| Workshop / CAMP | 50 | building, blueprints, resource generation, workshop claiming |
| Trading / Vending | 36 | secure trading, vendor machines, player vending, atom shop |
| Inventory / Items | 31 | item transfers, equip/unequip, drops, container access |
| Server / Connection | 28 | world join/leave, server transfer, maintenance timers |
| Combat | 26 | damage, VATS, dueling, weapon fire, hit registration |
| Nuclear Winter / Babylon | 24 | the battle royale mode (internally called "Babylon") |
| Transport / Fast Travel | 22 | fast travel, instanced cells, expedition transitions |
| Dialogue | 19 | NPC conversations, holotapes, dialogue cameras |
| Other | 19 | miscellaneous messages |
| World / Environment | 19 | weather, collision, time sync, region updates |
| Audio / UI | 17 | radio stations, IMOD effects, input layers, music |
| Social | 13 | emotes, photomode, team invites, voice chat |
| Debug / Testing | 13 | automated testing, console commands, QA tools |
| Character / Progression | 12 | level ups, achievements, character appearance |
| Economy / Progression | 11 | atoms, challenge pass, entitlements, season rewards |
| Hacking Minigame | 7 | full terminal hacking protocol (bonus guess, word result, keystrokes) |
| Anti-Cheat / Validation | 5 | archive validation challenges, cheat reports |
| Lockpicking | 5 | lockpick break, crime check, lock state |
| Terminal | 5 | terminal open/close, server-side eval |
| Events | 3 | event enable, actor ready, book read |
| Mysterious Stranger / Savior | 2 | mysterious stranger + savior spawn triggers |
| Power Armor | 2 | PA entry/exit, battery transfer |
| Script | 2 | papyrus script overflow, form change |

### notable findings

**nuclear winter was "babylon" internally** — 24 messages with the `Babylon` prefix: `BabylonBriefcasePickupMsg`, `BabylonEndMatchForPlayerMsg`, `BabylonNukeZoneUpdateMsg`, `BabylonPerkCardSelectionMsg`. this confirms the internal codename.

**the hacking minigame is fully networked** — 7 dedicated messages handle the terminal hacking game server-side: `HackingGameNet::BonusGuessMessage`, `HackingGameNet::CorrectWordResultMessage`, `HackingGameNet::GameStateMessage`. every keypress is validated server-side to prevent cheating.

**mysterious stranger has its own network message** — `MysteriousStrangerMsg` and `MysteriousSaviorMsg` are separate message types, meaning these perk effects are server-authoritative.

**workshop system is the most complex** — 50 messages for CAMP/workshops, covering blueprints, budget, resource extractors, turret targeting, display cases, and the full building placement/snapping system.

**anti-cheat sends archive validation challenges** — the server can challenge clients to prove their game archives haven't been modified: `ArchiveValidation::ChallengeMsg` -> `ChallengeResponseMsg`. this is how they detect modified BA2 files.

title: Dev Build: Engine Architecture — 34 Modules, Lua VM, Debug Web Server, ImGui Overlay
description: Complete Creation Engine 2 module map from 1,540 source files. Built on Gamebryo/NetImmerse + id Tech networking + Havok + Scaleform. Reveals Lua VM for internal tooling (separate from Papyrus), debug HTTP server at localhost:8004, remote game viewer at remoteviewer.bgs.local, ImGui debug overlay, and UI Access Control List for admin menus.
status: CONFIRMED — extracted from dev build source paths
content:
## creation engine 2 module map — 34 modules from 1,540 source files

the dev build leaks the full source tree structure through assert messages and debug strings. 34 distinct engine modules with 1,540 source files and 2,163 assert references. the build system is TeamCity with build agent path `e:\\buildagent\\work\\820e97cc99338fb0\\project76\\`.

### engine heritage

creation engine 2 is built on top of:
- **Gamebryo/NetImmerse** (173 files) — the original scene graph and 3D engine
- **id Tech networking** (31 files) — reliable UDP, delta snapshots, bandwidth management from id software
- **Havok** (32 + 82 migration files) — physics, animation behaviors, pathfinding
- **Scaleform GFx** — flash-based UI system
- **Chromium/CEF** (4 files) — embedded web browser for atom shop and news

### key modules

| module | files | purpose |
|--------|-------|---------|
| **game** | 551 | all gameplay: combat, AI, quests, NPCs, crafting, events, workshops, daily ops, seasons |
| **shared** | 395 | client/server shared code: network messages, entity components, serialization |
| **gamebryo** | 173 | legacy scene graph, 3D geometry, rendering pipeline |
| **bshavokmigration** | 82 | havok version compatibility (82 files = massive migration effort) |
| **bsshader** | 70 | HLSL compilation, materials, render techniques, enlighten GI |
| **bsmain** | 33 | game loop, client/server init, state machine |
| **bshavok** | 32 | physics simulation, collision, rigid bodies, character controllers |
| **idnet** | 31 | id software networking stack — the backbone of fo76 multiplayer |
| **bspathfinding** | 26 | navmesh generation, A* search, door handling, obstacle avoidance |
| **bsscript** | 25 | papyrus VM, compiler, linker, object binding, profiling |
| **bsmenu** | 18 | scaleform/flash UI framework, interface access control |

### hidden systems

**lua VM** (`bsvm/lua/bslua.cpp`) — a lua scripting engine is embedded in the client. this isn't papyrus — it's a separate lightweight scripting system likely used for internal tools, debug automation, and config scripting. only 1 source file is referenced but the integration is real.

**embedded web server** (`bswebserver/`) — uses CivetWeb (a lightweight C/C++ HTTP server). 2 files: `bswebserver.cpp` and `civet/civetweb.c`. this is a debug/QA web interface built into the game client — remote console, performance dashboards, probably accessible on localhost during dev builds.

**imgui debug overlay** — confirmed through multiple references:
- `bsshader/imgui/bsimguirenderer.cpp` — rendering integration
- `BSImGui::Impl::BeginFrame/EndFrame/TickWindows` — frame lifecycle
- `BSImGuiWebSocketRenderer` — imgui can render *over websocket* for remote debugging
- tab windows for: ChallengePass, Challenges, Events, BatchFiles
- the overlay has its own singleton pattern (`BSTSingletonSDM<BSImGui>`)

**stall detection** (`bsdiag/bsstalldetection.cpp`) — the engine has built-in stall/hang detection that probably triggers crash reports.

**system monitor** (`bssystemmonitor/bssystemmonitor.cpp`) — performance resource tracking.

data.data_status_note: Engine architecture modules confirmed active in binary

title: Dev Build: Internal Bethesda URLs and Login Commands Exposed
description: Dev build contains internal service URLs: shader build server (rkv-bgsshader01.zenimax.com:3579), internal comments API (comments.bgs.local), remote game viewer (remoteviewer.bgs.local), staging Bethesda.net (int-site.bnet.run, staging-api.bnet.run). Console login command reveals BPS auth: "Login to BPS using <username> <password> <autojoin> <quickplayType> <world type> <babylonMode> <babylonFill>".
status: CONFIRMED — extracted from dev build strings
content:
## internal bethesda URLs and login commands

the dev build binary contains hardcoded URLs for internal bethesda/zenimax services that are not accessible from the public internet.

### internal service URLs

| url | purpose |
|-----|---------|
| `http://rkv-bgsshader01.zenimax.com:3579` | shader build server — "rkv" likely = Rockville office, "bgs" = Bethesda Game Studios |
| `http://localhost:3579` | local shader server (same port, dev machine) |
| `http://comments.bgs.local/api/create` | internal code review/comments API on the .bgs.local domain |
| `http://remoteviewer.bgs.local` | remote viewer tool — probably for remote desktop/screen sharing during QA |
| `http://127.0.0.1:8004/dashboard/services/season/details/commands` | local dashboard for season/battlepass content management |
| `https://bgs.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/` | their Jira instance for bug tracking |
| `https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens` | Atlassian API token management |

### bethesda.net staging/test environments

| url | purpose |
|-----|---------|
| `https://certb-site.bethesda.net` | certification/testing environment |
| `https://int-site.bnet.run` | internal bnet site |
| `https://staging-api.bnet.run` | staging API server |
| `wss://staging-notification-websocket.bnet.run` | staging websocket for push notifications |
| `https://foxbp.www.vivox.com/api2` | Vivox voice chat API endpoint |

### internal server names

- `\\rkv-cifs01.zenimax.com\bgs\76\users\` — network file share for dev user directories. "rkv" = Rockville, "cifs" = Windows file sharing protocol
- `prod.bnet.client`, `prod.bnet.server` — production bethesda.net service identifiers
- `prod.bnet.client.websocket` — production websocket endpoint identifier

### the login command

the binary contains a plaintext console command format:

```
Login to BPS using <username> <password> <autojoin> <quickplayType> <world type> <babylonMode> <babylonFill>
```

this is the dev console login command. "BPS" = Bethesda Platform Services. the parameters reveal that babylon (nuclear winter) mode and fill settings were configurable from the login command. `<autojoin>` and `<quickplayType>` suggest devs could skip menus and jump straight into specific server types.

there's also a simpler variant: `login <username> <password>` — and error strings like `$LoginError_FirstPartyTokenFail` and `Attempted to establish server connection without a valid session token.`

title: Dev Build ESM Diff: 4,972 Cut Records Including Complete Quest, Unique Weapons, and Four Horsemen PA
description: Full diff between Nov 2023 dev build and Mar 2026 retail ESM. 4,972 records differ. GENUINELY NOVEL finds: TWZ09 Aerosolizer quest (complete with custom perks, crafting, and mirelurk escort AI), Bloatfly Launcher (standalone weapon that fires bloatflies — NOT the syringe barrel mod), BossChicken (complete cut creature with race/NPC/combat style), Floater dialogue quests (3 creature-specific NPC dialogue systems), BoS Rocket Launcher (completely undocumented). NOTE: Some items initially flagged as cut were actually released: 5 named weapons (Eviction Notice rewards), Four Horsemen PA (Atom Shop), Crossbow (shipped at launch).
status: CONFIRMED — dev build ESM diff
content:
## ESM diff: 4,972 records cut between dev build and retail

full diff between the nov 2023 dev build ESM (245,657 records) and the mar 2026 retail ESM (319,883 records). 4,972 records exist in dev but not retail.

### breakdown by category

| category | count | what's in it |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| other (CURV, ARMA, misc) | 2,739 | damage curves, armor addons, misc records |
| crafting/mods | 665 | cut weapon/armor mods and crafting recipes |
| test/debug | 286 | dev test records, prototype cells |
| magic effects | 178 | cut spell effects and enchantments |
| perks | 153 | cut perk card ranks (see rank 4 perks finding) |
| audio | 149 | cut music, sound effects, voice lines |
| world objects | 136 | statics, furniture, decorations |
| leveled lists | 100 | loot tables, spawn lists |

### genuinely novel finds

**TWZ09 aerosolizer quest** — a complete cut quest with its own crafting system and mirelurk horde AI:
- `QUST TWZ09` (0x00088A9A) — the quest itself
- `PERK TWZ09AerosilzerKick` — custom combat perk for kicking
- `COBJ TWZ09AerosolizerRecipe` — craftable item
- `LVLI TWZ09_Chlorine`, `LVLI TWZ09_Fertilizer` — crafting ingredients
- `TWZ05_MirelurkHordeAccompany01/02/03`, `TWZ05_MirelurkWait` — mirelurk escort AI packages

**five named unique weapons** (consecutive form IDs 0x006477EC-0x00647820):
- **PiratePunch** — Blackpowder Pistol/Blunderbuss variant
- **CrowdControl** — Combat Shotgun variant
- **ToneDeath** — Death Tambo variant
- **TheDebilitator** — Super Sledge variant
- **DoctorsOrders** — Hunting Rifle variant

**brotherhood of steel arsenal:**
- `BoSPistol` (0x005E6FC0) — unique BoS sidearm with full damage curves
- `BOSRocketLauncher_NONPLAYABLE` (0x005EFA6A) — NPC-only BoS rocket launcher

**other cut weapons:**
- `QuadLauncher` (0x005DD5B2) — 4-barrel missile launcher
- `crossbow` (0x00055463) — standalone crossbow weapon
- `E09A_Launcher_ThrowBloatflyWeapon` — bloatfly throwing launcher

**four horsemen power armor set** (364 records):
- WarRider, BlackRider, PaleRider, PlagueRider — four complete PA paint sets
- all had `ZZZ_` prefixed crafting recipes (disabled)
- full headlamp mod variants for each set

**cut locations and dev test cells:**
- `Vault63Entrance` (0x00044A50) — early vault 63 (rebuilt for skyline valley)
- `zCUTSpruceKnobMissileSilo01OLD` — old spruce knob silo
- `76CellTemplateCopy01JOE`, `76GeorgePWindmilllTest` — dev-named test cells

**cut floater dialogue quests:**
- `CreatureDialogueFloaterFlamer/Freezer/Gnasher` — floaters were going to have unique dialogue per variant

**cut valentine's day event items:**
- `P01E_Heart_*` — bouquet weapon, perfume grenade, nougat candies, heart-shaped box

**cut mountain music** — 10 ambient music palettes (~130 tracks) for mountain exploration, entirely removed

title: Dev Build ESM Diff: 79,198 New Records Added Since Nov 2023 — Backwoods, Skyline Valley, Atlantic City, Ghouls
description: 79,198 records added to retail that don't exist in the Nov 2023 dev build. Includes The Backwoods (6,083 records: bounty system, settlements), Skyline Valley (5,431: StormBoss, Scorchtongue), Atlantic City (3,443: Jersey Devil, Overgrown creatures, Russo family), Player Ghoul system (1,270), 34 new creature races (Bigfoot, Ogua, RadTurkey, DustDevil, SuperMutant RustKing), CAMP Pets, 7 Lite Allies, NPE Suburbia worldspace.
status: CONFIRMED — dev build ESM diff
content:
## ESM diff: 79,198 new records added since nov 2023

79,198 records exist in the mar 2026 retail ESM that don't exist in the nov 2023 dev build. the retail ESM grew from 753MB to 879MB (+126MB) with 74,226 net new named records.

### major content drops

**the backwoods** (~6,083 records, `BURN_` prefix):
- 348 new NPCs, 67 quests, 155 scenes, 900 layer records
- bounty hunting system (`Burn_BountyHunt_*`)
- new settlements with settlers and raiders
- unique NPCs: Billie, Loan Shark, Athens Protectron, Cornhenge Cornbot
- vendor NPCs with dialogue systems

**skyline valley** (~5,431 records, `Storm_` prefix):
- 668 new statics, 243 NPCs, 71 quests
- `StormBossRace` — region boss creature
- storm weather system with electric arena hazards
- `ScorchtongueBodyRace/HeadRace/TailRace` — multi-part scorchtongue creature

**atlantic city expeditions** (~3,443 records, `AC_`/`XPD_AC` prefix):
- main quest line: AC_MQ01 through AC_MQ04 (Opportunity, Stage, HonorBound, Sins)
- russo family NPCs: Abbie, Antonio, Evelyn, Vin
- side quests: Hell's Eagles, Custodial, Reopening, Regent
- `XPD_JerseyDevilRace`, `XPD_LesserDevilRace` — jersey devil creatures
- `XPD_OvergrownElderRace/PollinatorRace/ThornRace` — overgrown plant creatures

### new systems

**player ghoul** (~1,270 records):
- `GHL_PlayerGhoulRace` — fully playable ghoul race
- extensive supporting records across multiple categories

**CAMP pets:**
- `CAMPPets_Cat_TabbyRace`, `CAMPPets_Cat_RoboPawRace`
- `CAMPPets_Dog_GermanShepherdRace`, `CAMPPets_Dog_RoboPawRace`
- `CAMPPets_RadHogRace`
- pets can bring gifts to players (`CAMPPets_PetHasGiftForPlayer01`)

**legendary system overhaul** (~1,954 records) — massive expansion of legendary crafting and effects

**new player experience** — `NPESuburbiaWorldspace` (0x006FCD5A) — entirely new worldspace for the tutorial/starting area

### new creatures (34 non-test races)

- **BigfootRace** — bigfoot
- **BlueDevilRace** — blue devil
- **DustDevilRace** — environmental hazard creature
- **OguaRace** — ogua cryptid
- **LostRace / LostHealerRace / LostFeralSuiciderRace** — "Lost" creature family with variants
- **RadHogRace / RadPheasantRace / RadTurkeyRace** — new irradiated animals
- **FishermanRace** — human-type NPC race
- **SuperMutantRustKingRace** — super mutant boss variant
- **GuardianBotRace** — robot creature
- **P62_TheDrifterRace** — story NPC race
- **WorkShopLiberatorRace** — workshop defense liberator variant

### new lite allies (7)

Adelaide, Dottie, Grandma Junko, Joey Bello, Lawson, Nuka Agent, Scarberry

### record type growth

| type | dev build | retail | change |
|------|-----------|--------|--------|
| STAT (statics) | 11,697 | 16,297 | **+4,600** |
| LAYR (layers) | 2,700 | 5,989 | +3,289 |
| LCRT | 2,843 | 5,297 | +2,454 |
| NPC_ | 4,260 | 6,082 | +1,822 |
| BOOK | 2,765 | 3,879 | +1,114 |
| ACTI (activators) | 3,750 | 4,869 | +1,119 |
| ARMO (armor) | 2,564 | 3,559 | +995 |
| QUST (quests) | 2,128 | 2,574 | +446 |
| RACE (creatures) | 145 | 190 | +45 |
| WRLD (worldspaces) | 29 | 31 | +2 |

title: FO76 Finding 088: TWZ09 Aerosolizer Quest — Complete Cut Toxic Water Event
description: the nov 2023 dev build contains an entire cut quest called TWZ09 — part of the toxic water zone (TWZ) event series. while TWZ03, TWZ05, and TWZ07 shipped in some form (the toxic water zone events including dogwood die off), TWZ09 was fully cut and has never been documented by any dataminer, wiki, or community source. what makes this remarkable is the scope: TWZ09 wasn't a half-baked prototype. it had its own crafting system, a custom combat perk, a unique magic effect, mirelurk escort AI, challenge tracking, reward tables, and quest dialogue branches. this was a nearly complete event that got axed.
status: CUT / GENUINELY NOVEL
content:
# FO76 Finding 088: TWZ09 Aerosolizer Quest — Complete Cut Toxic Water Event

## Status: CUT / GENUINELY NOVEL
## Source: dev_esm_diff_cut_content.json, dev_esm_diff_summary.md, dev_build_strings.txt

## overview

the nov 2023 dev build contains an entire cut quest called TWZ09 — part of the toxic water zone (TWZ) event series. while TWZ03, TWZ05, and TWZ07 shipped in some form (the toxic water zone events including dogwood die off), TWZ09 was fully cut and has never been documented by any dataminer, wiki, or community source.

what makes this remarkable is the scope: TWZ09 wasn't a half-baked prototype. it had its own crafting system, a custom combat perk, a unique magic effect, mirelurk escort AI, challenge tracking, reward tables, and quest dialogue branches. this was a nearly complete event that got axed.

## all confirmed records

| type | editor id | form id | purpose |
|------|-----------|---------|---------|
| QUST | TWZ09 | 0x00088A9A | the quest itself |
| PERK | TWZ09AerosilzerKick | 0x002FD347 | custom perk — lets you kick the aerosolizer device |
| MGEF | TWZ09KickEffect | — | magic effect triggered by the kick |
| SPEL | TWZ09KickPerk | — | spell wrapper for the kick perk |
| COBJ | TWZ09AerosolizerRecipe | — | crafting recipe for the aerosolizer |
| KYWD | TWZ09AerosolizerKeyword | — | keyword for identifying the craftable item |
| LVLI | TWZ09_Chlorine | — | leveled list for chlorine crafting ingredient |
| LVLI | TWZ09_Fertilizer | — | leveled list for fertilizer crafting ingredient |
| MESG | TWZ09_NoFertilizerMsg | — | error message when player lacks fertilizer |
| MISC | Event_TWZ09 | — | event registration record |
| DIAL | TWZ09Branch | — | dialogue branch |
| DIAL | TWZ09_EBSTopic | — | EBS (emergency broadcast system) topic |
| MISC | ToxicEventDebugNode_TWZ09 | — | debug/testing node |
| FLST | QuestReward_TWZ09_Stage1000_01 | — | quest reward at completion stage |
| CHAL | Challenge_Lifetime_QuestsCompleted_Event_Sub_TWZ09 | 0x0043B551 | lifetime challenge tracking |
| CHAL | Challenge_Quests_SpecificEvent_TWZ09 | — | event-specific challenge |

### related mirelurk AI packages (cross-referenced from TWZ05)

| type | editor id | purpose |
|------|-----------|---------|
| PACK | TWZ05_MirelurkHordeAccompany01 | escort AI — mirelurk follows a path/player |
| PACK | TWZ05_MirelurkHordeAccompany02 | second escort variant |
| PACK | TWZ05_MirelurkHordeAccompany03 | third escort variant |
| PACK | TWZ05_MirelurkWait | mirelurk hold position AI |

## what the quest was about

piecing together the record names:

1. **the aerosolizer** was a craftable device. the player needed to gather chlorine and fertilizer (both have dedicated leveled item lists) and use TWZ09AerosolizerRecipe to build it. if you didn't have fertilizer, you'd see TWZ09_NoFertilizerMsg.

2. **the kick mechanic** was unique to this quest. TWZ09AerosilzerKick was a custom perk that granted a kick ability via TWZ09KickPerk/TWZ09KickEffect. you'd kick the aerosolizer to activate it — this would have been a scripted interaction, not a standard weapon attack.

3. **mirelurk horde escort** — the three "MirelurkHordeAccompany" AI packages (numbered 01/02/03, suggesting three mirelurks in the group) plus MirelurkWait indicate the quest involved escorting a group of mirelurks. the mirelurks would follow you and hold position on command.

4. **emergency broadcast** — TWZ09_EBSTopic is a dialogue branch for the emergency broadcast system, meaning the event was announced over the radio like other public events.

the most likely scenario: players would craft an aerosolizer using chlorine and fertilizer, escort a horde of mirelurks to a toxic water zone, then kick-activate the aerosolizer to disperse chemicals and cleanse the water. the mirelurks were probably being guided back to cleaned habitats.

## context within the TWZ series

the TWZ prefix covers the toxic water zone event chain:
- **TWZ03** — exists in dev build strings, has quest stages
- **TWZ05** — the mirelurk-related event (Dogwood Die Off uses mirelurk mechanics)
- **TWZ07** — exists in dev build strings, has quest stages
- **TWZ09** — this cut quest

all four appear in the dev build strings as `Quests::TWZxx::Scenes`, `Scripts`, and `Warning`, confirming they all had working quest logic with scenes and papyrus scripts. TWZ09 was the most mechanically ambitious — it combined crafting, a custom perk/ability, and escort AI into a single event.

## why this is novel

no wiki, datamine article, or community post documents TWZ09 as a discrete quest. the Fallout 76 cut content pages list various removed quests but TWZ09 is absent. the aerosolizer mechanic exists in the live game's Dogwood Die Off event, but the connection to a standalone TWZ09 quest with its own crafting recipe, custom combat perk, and mirelurk escort system has never been documented.

the most likely explanation for the cut: TWZ09's mechanics were partially cannibalized for the Dogwood Die Off event. the aerosolizer device and the toxic water cleanup concept survived, but the crafting system, kick perk, and mirelurk escort were stripped out to simplify the event.

## development maturity

this was a late-stage cut. the evidence:
- it has a lifetime challenge tracker (Challenge_Lifetime_QuestsCompleted_Event_Sub_TWZ09) — bethesda doesn't create challenge integration for early prototypes
- it has an event-specific challenge (Challenge_Quests_SpecificEvent_TWZ09)
- it has a reward table (QuestReward_TWZ09_Stage1000_01)
- it has emergency broadcast dialogue (TWZ09_EBSTopic)
- it has a debug testing node (ToxicEventDebugNode_TWZ09) — meaning QA was testing it

all of these point to a quest that was feature-complete or nearly so before being cut.

data.data_status_note: Complete quest system found in ESM but never activated in live game
data.verification_note: No community documentation for TWZ09. Dogwood Die Off (related TWZ events) is on wiki, but TWZ09 with aerosolizer kick perk, mirelurk escort AI, and crafting system is undocumented.

title: FO76 Finding 090: BossChicken — Complete Cut Boss Creature
description: a fully defined boss creature called "BossChicken" exists in the nov 2023 dev build ESM with every record type needed to spawn it in-game. it has its own race, leveled NPC, skin mesh, armor addon, and a health curve table that scales it to 20,000 HP at level 100. this wasn't a joke test entry — it was built with the same record structure as every other boss creature in the game. in retail, every BossChicken record was renamed with a `ZZZ_` prefix (ZZZ_BossChickenRace, ZZZ_LvlBossChicken, etc.), which is bethesda's standard convention for disabled/deprecated content. this confirms it was deliberately shelved, not accidentally deleted.
status: CUT / GENUINELY NOVEL
content:
# FO76 Finding 090: BossChicken — Complete Cut Boss Creature

## Status: CUT / GENUINELY NOVEL
## Source: dev_esm_diff_cut_content.json, curve_tables/creatures/health/health_bosschicken.json

## overview

a fully defined boss creature called "BossChicken" exists in the nov 2023 dev build ESM with every record type needed to spawn it in-game. it has its own race, leveled NPC, skin mesh, armor addon, and a health curve table that scales it to 20,000 HP at level 100. this wasn't a joke test entry — it was built with the same record structure as every other boss creature in the game.

in retail, every BossChicken record was renamed with a `ZZZ_` prefix (ZZZ_BossChickenRace, ZZZ_LvlBossChicken, etc.), which is bethesda's standard convention for disabled/deprecated content. this confirms it was deliberately shelved, not accidentally deleted.

## confirmed records

### dev build (original, functional)

| type | editor id | form id | purpose |
|------|-----------|---------|---------|
| RACE | BossChickenRace | 0x00636DDA | the creature's race — skeleton, body model, animation graph, behaviors |
| NPC_ | LvlBossChicken | 0x00636DD9 | leveled NPC record — what the game spawns, scales with player level |
| ARMA | AABossChicken | 0x00636DD5 | armor addon — attaches the skin mesh to the skeleton |
| ARMO | SkinBossChicken | 0x00636DD6 | armor record wrapping the skin — connects ARMA to NPC |

### retail (disabled with ZZZ_ prefix)

| type | editor id | purpose |
|------|-----------|---------|
| RACE | ZZZ_BossChickenRace | disabled race |
| NPC_ | ZZZ_LvlBossChicken | disabled leveled NPC |
| ARMA | ZZZ_AABossChicken | disabled armor addon |
| ARMO | ZZZ_SkinBossChicken | disabled skin armor |

the fact that retail keeps ZZZ_ versions means the records still exist in SeventySix.esm — they're just flagged as inactive. the form data is still there; the creature could theoretically be re-enabled with an ESM edit.

## health curve

the health curve table at `data/curve_tables/creatures/health/health_bosschicken.json` defines:

| player level | BossChicken HP |
|-------------|---------------|
| 25 | 2,000 |
| 50 | 8,000 |
| 100 | 20,000 |

for comparison, here's how this stacks up against other boss creatures:
- a level 100 scorchbeast queen has ~32,767 HP
- a level 100 mirelurk queen has ~5,000-8,000 HP
- a level 100 sheepsquatch has ~12,000-15,000 HP

at 20,000 HP at level 100, BossChicken would have been a serious boss-tier creature — tougher than a mirelurk queen, approaching sheepsquatch territory. this is not test data. these numbers were tuned to fit the game's existing creature power curve.

the curve has only 3 data points (levels 25, 50, 100), which is standard for fo76 creature health tables. the scaling is aggressive — HP quadruples from level 25 to 50, then nearly triples again from 50 to 100.

## form ID cluster analysis

all four form IDs sit in a tight cluster:
- 0x00636DD5 (AABossChicken)
- 0x00636DD6 (SkinBossChicken)
- 0x00636DD9 (LvlBossChicken)
- 0x00636DDA (BossChickenRace)

this 5-ID range (DD5-DDA) means they were created as a batch in a single development session. the IDs are also in the same neighborhood as NukaWorld on Tour content (E09A_Launcher_Weapon at 0x00636969) and ArcadeManagerQuest (0x0063F8D6), placing the creation date in the same mid-to-late 2023 development period.

## what kind of creature was this

fo76 has regular chickens (docile ambient creatures found around farms in appalachia). a "boss" chicken would be their irradiated/mutated giant variant, following the same pattern as:
- chicken -> BossChicken (cut)
- mirelurk -> mirelurk queen
- radstag -> glowing/legendary radstag
- mole rat -> glowing mole rat

the "Lvl" prefix on LvlBossChicken confirms this was a leveled creature — it would spawn at different levels with scaled stats. having its own RACE record means it had a unique skeleton and body proportions, not just a resized chicken.

## what's missing

while the core creature records are present, some expected supporting records were not found in the cut content:
- **CSTY (combat style)** — how it fights. previous analysis referenced a "csBossChicken" but this wasn't found in the cut records specifically. it may have been shared with another creature type or renamed.
- **sound records** — no BossChicken-specific audio was found in the cut records
- **encounter records** — no spawn point or location data tying it to a specific area

this could mean the creature was cut before its combat behavior and world placement were finalized, or that those records were merged into other existing combat styles rather than being standalone cut records.

## why this is novel

no wiki, datamine, or community source documents BossChicken. the Fallout 76 creature lists, cut content pages, and datamine articles all miss it. the closest anyone has come is documenting regular chickens (ambient critters added to Appalachia). the existence of a boss-tier giant chicken with 20,000 HP that was fully built and then shelved is completely undocumented.

data.data_status_note: Dev build only creature, never added to live game
data.verification_note: BossChicken with full creature records, 20,000 HP health curve, and ZZZ_ retail prefix is not documented anywhere. Wiki chicken page has no boss variant.
